name: wmkf_proposalbudgetline
description: Intake-portal slice 0. Per-year, per-category budget rows drained from the applicant intake portal, child of akoya_request (parental, cascade delete). TWO DISTINCT DELETE AXES — do not conflate (Connor S162 ruling, see docs/INTAKE_PORTAL_ITEM_6_DISCUSSION.md § 0 'Update 2026-05-18 (S163)'): (1) the parental cascade.Delete:Cascade below governs WHOLE-akoya_request deletion / orphan cleanup — stays as specced; (2) the drain's post-submit-edit reconciliation must DEACTIVATE obsolete rows (statecode->Inactive), NEVER hard-delete them DURING DRAIN/POST-SUBMIT RECONCILIATION — the Item-6 recompute fires on that child Update and sums ACTIVE children only. (Whole-akoya_request deletion via axis (1)'s parental cascade DOES hard-delete children — that is an administrative/retention path, out of scope for the recompute flow since the parent is gone; it is not a counterexample to the deactivate rule, which scopes to drain reconciliation only.) PARENT-HARD-DELETE POLICY (Codex review S163): whole-akoya_request hard delete is PERMITTED ONLY as a deliberate human administrative / data-retention operation; it MUST NOT be invoked by any automated drain, reconciliation, or recompute path. No automated code path in this codebase may hard-delete an akoya_request or rely on the child cascade for cleanup. Deactivation needs NO schema here: Dataverse custom entities carry statecode/statuscode by default. Cost-share rows live here too via wmkf_category 100000006-100000008 — WMKF-spend aggregate queries must filter wmkf_category NOT IN (100000006,100000007,100000008). Entity name LOCKED as wmkf_proposalbudgetline (Justin decision S163, 2026-05-18 — the wmkf_budgetline alternative is dropped; was flagged for Connor naming review, now closed). Authoritative spec: docs/BUDGET_FORM_SPEC.md v3 + docs/INTAKE_PORTAL_SCHEMA_CHANGES.md 2026-05-14 entry.

primaryNameAttribute.description: Synthesized display string: Y{year} — {category}: {description}. PA cover-doc builder may regenerate its own grouping strings; this is the picker/grid label.

attributes.description: Program year this line belongs to (1-based). Integer not Choice — forward-compatible across program lengths.

Budget category. 100000000-100000005 are WMKF-spend (Indirect reserved, always $0). 100000006-100000008 are cost-share — excluded from the WMKF-requested aggregate, summed into akoya_request.wmkf_totalothersources. Integer values reserved 2026-05-14 (S150); DO NOT renumber after deploy — Connor PAs / drain guards / packet builder hardcode these. LABELS match docs/INTAKE_PORTAL_SCHEMA_CHANGES.md:73-81 verbatim. Cost-share labels NORMALIZED to spaced form 'Waived Indirect'/'Waived Tuition'/'Other Cost Share' (Justin decision S163, 2026-05-18 — resolves the prior camelCase-vs-spaced inconsistency; was flagged as a Connor review item, now closed). NOTE: filter-predicate shorthand in BUDGET_FORM_SPEC / ITEM_6 / SCHEMA_REVIEW docs still writes the old camelCase tokens (WaivedIndirect etc.) — those are integer-backed CATEGORY references, NOT the display label, and the integers are authoritative for every guard, so they are intentionally left as semantic shorthand.

USD amount for this line. One value per row (the row IS one year). Negative values rejected by the drain server-side before createRecord — Dataverse Money MinValue is set to 0 here but the drain is the authoritative guard (docs/INTAKE_PORTAL_SCHEMA_CHANGES.md 2026-05-14 'Drain server-side validation').
Fixed-row discriminator for catalog rows only (principal-investigators, consumable-supplies, facilities-overhead); null for dynamic rows.
Personnel-only (wmkf_category = 100000000 Personnel); null otherwise. Whole-number percent 0-100.

relationships.lookupDescription: The proposal this budget line belongs to. Parental: deleting the REQUEST cascades delete to its budget lines (whole-proposal orphan cleanup only). NOTE: the drain's post-submit-edit reconciliation does NOT delete individual lines — it deactivates them (statecode->Inactive) per Connor S162; see entity description.
